ksa_text: Skill in designing and implementing data anonymization and pseudonymization techniques appropriate to the intended use, including k-anonymity, differential privacy, and synthetic data generation approaches.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in applying confidentiality, integrity, and availability principles.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of data concealment tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of data concealment tools and techniques
Skill in applying confidentiality, integrity, and availability principles.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA SP-S-005 (Security and Privacy/Skill) excluded as out_of_scope (composite 0.399, highest: NICE at 0.399). Domain prior: out. Core hits: 0. OOS hits: 0. Seniority: appropriate. Data quality framework management, DQ metrics/profiling/monitoring, quality rules, remediation oversight, and cross-functional quality standards at a manager level.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Exclude consistent with out_of_scope classification, composite 0.399, and Security and Privacy (SP) handling.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence challenge the exclusion of this Security and Privacy KSA (composite 0.399)?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Market analysis of Data Quality Manager job postings does not surface this Security and Privacy competency as a standard requirement at the relevant level. Exclusion consistent with role focus.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Exclusion upheld. Market evidence consistent with exclusion. Final decision: exclude.
